title: Explainable AI for Credit Risk Assessment in Vietnamese Commercial Banks
abstract: This paper presents an explainable AI framework for credit risk assessment tailored to Vietnamese commercial banking contexts, combining gradient boosting with SHAP-based interpretability.

title: Digital Transformation Maturity Assessment Framework for SMEs in Emerging Markets
abstract: We propose a five-stage digital transformation maturity model validated through survey data from 312 Vietnamese SMEs, providing actionable roadmaps for practitioners.

title: Blockchain-Based Supply Chain Transparency for Agricultural Products in Vietnam
abstract: A blockchain prototype for agricultural supply chain traceability is developed and piloted with three cooperatives in northern Vietnam, achieving 94% stakeholder satisfaction.

title: Sentiment Analysis of Customer Reviews for Vietnamese E-Commerce Platforms Using PhoBERT
abstract: We fine-tune PhoBERT for Vietnamese aspect-based sentiment analysis on a novel dataset of 50,000 reviews scraped from Shopee and Tiki, outperforming existing baselines by 4.3% F1.

title: Knowledge Graph Embedding for Product Recommendation in Retail
abstract: A knowledge graph-augmented collaborative filtering model is proposed for retail product recommendation, leveraging entity relationships to address cold-start challenges.

title: Deep Learning Approaches for Financial Fraud Detection: A Systematic Review
abstract: This systematic review analyzes 127 deep learning papers on financial fraud detection published 2018–2023, identifying key architectures, datasets, and open challenges.

title: A Hybrid LSTM-Attention Model for Stock Price Prediction in Emerging Markets
venue: ICIS 2022 – International Conference on Information Systems
abstract: We propose a hybrid LSTM-attention architecture for short-term stock price prediction on the Ho Chi Minh Stock Exchange, achieving superior RMSE over baseline models.

title: Factors Influencing Mobile Payment Adoption Among Vietnamese Millennials
venue: Tạp chí Kinh tế và Phát triển (Journal of Economics and Development)
abstract: Using a TAM-extended model, we survey 450 Vietnamese millennials to identify the key determinants of mobile payment adoption, with perceived security and social influence as top predictors.
